title: Taxi

vocabulary.meaning: the money paid for a taxi ride
the person who drives the taxi
a device that shows the cost of the journey
the act of collecting a passenger
ready to be used or chosen

questions: What comes to mind when you hear the word 'taxi'?
How often do you take a taxi?
Do you have any 'taxi stories'?
Are there enough taxi cabs in your town?
What do you think of taxis in other countries?
Do you think it's always safe to use a taxi?
Are taxis good value for money?
Which do you like better, New York's yellow cabs or London's black cabs?
How do you flag (U.S.) or hail (U.K.) a taxi in your country?
What would happen in your town if all the taxis disappeared?
Do you like taxis?
Do you think taxi drivers should know every road?
What's the worst taxi drive you've ever had?
Do you think a taxi driver's job is dangerous?
Why do taxi driver's always talk about the weather?
Do you always make sure the meter is on in the taxi?
Have you ever taken a taxi that took the long way round?
What kind of license do you think taxi drivers should have?
Do you think taxis should have 'in-taxi' entertainment?
Is it better to sit in the back of the taxi or in the passenger seat?
